params.text:
How to correct an error properly

Everyone makes the occasional mistake in a record. What matters is correcting it honestly and transparently, so the original entry can still be seen. On a paper record:

  • Draw a single line through the incorrect words — leave them still readable.
  • Write the correction alongside or on the next line.
  • Initial and date the change (add the time too), and a brief reason if helpful.

Never erase, scribble over, cover up, or use correction fluid — hiding an entry looks like you are concealing something and destroys the record's legal value. On electronic records, don't delete: the system keeps an audit trail, so add a correcting entry and let the timestamp and your login show who amended it and when.

params.question: You realise you wrote the wrong time on a paper daily note. What is the correct way to fix it?

answers.text: Draw a single line through the error so it can still be read, write the correction, then initial and date it.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. The original stays visible and it is clear who changed it and when.

answers.text: Cover the mistake with correction fluid and write the right time over the top.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Never use correction fluid. Hiding an entry destroys the record's integrity and looks like concealment.

answers.text: Scribble it out heavily so the wrong time can't be read at all.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: The original entry must remain legible. Use a single line, not a scribble.

answers.text: Rip out the page and rewrite the whole note cleanly.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Never remove or destroy pages. Correct the entry in place so the full history is preserved.
